skillName: Recognise the place value of each digit in whole numbers up to millions
misconceptionSummary: Students struggle with zeros as place holders and mis-read the number of digits.

steps.narration: Look at all the zeros in this number. They aren't mistakes — each one is doing an important job. Without them, the other digits would slip into the wrong columns.

visuals.ruleText: A zero holds a column open
visuals.subText: It means: no groups of this size — but the column still exists

steps.narration: Think of a zero as a seat-saver. The column is occupied even though no groups of that size are present. Remove the zero and everything shifts — changing the number completely.
Count every digit from the right — zeros included. The zeros keep four and three in their correct positions. Skip a zero and you've misread the whole number.

visuals.label: Zeros are place holders — always count them

steps.narration: The three is in the millions column because four zeros are keeping it there. Never skip zeros when counting positions from the right.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: ignoring zeros when counting digit positions, which shifts every other digit into the wrong column.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: When you see zeros, count them — don't skip them. Each one holds a column open for the digits around it.
